Sequence of Returns Simulator

Monte Carlo analysis of returns, inflation, and dynamic withdrawals.

Investment Horizon

000s
Advanced simulation settings
%

Averages a band of runs around each percentile (P5–P60) to reduce noise. 0 = a single run.

Adds year-to-year persistence by drawing runs of consecutive years averaging this length (Historical Resampling and Smoothed Historical); set to 1 for fully independent years. Log-Normal uses it for year-to-year smoothing instead.

Softens Smoothed Historical's output so it isn't limited to only the exact years in your selected range. Not used by the other two methods.

How many simulations Goal Seek runs to score each candidate plan while searching. Lower = faster search but noisier results; higher = slower but more reliable. The final plan is always confirmed using the full Number of Simulations above.

Future Returns

Select the range of years to use as basis for simulating future returns.

Withdrawal Strategy

000s

In today's dollars — the simulator keeps purchasing power constant, so don't build in inflation raises.

Spending Over Time

Bias spending earlier in retirement. The annual real change scales the whole withdrawal each year (negative = spend more now, less later); the early-years bonus adds a flat amount for the first few years (the "go-go years").

%
000s
Minimum withdrawal

Always withdraw at least this amount each year (capped by portfolio balance), even if your schedule above would go lower — the chart below reflects this floor. Add tiers to stage different minimums over time — the last tier applies to all remaining years. Remove all tiers for no minimum. Doesn't apply to a Specific List.

Paste from a spreadsheet — new lines, tabs, commas, semicolons, or pipes. Negative values are deposits. Last value is reused for remaining years.

Dynamic Adjustments & Guardrails

Market adjustment

Adjust withdrawal based on nominal market return (see chart). When balance is below the Low threshold, that adjustment always applies. When balance is above Expected or High, those adjustments only raise spending on bad market years — they never reduce it after a good year.

Low Return
%
000s

blank = off

000s
Expected
%
000s

blank = off

000s
High Return
%
000s

blank = off

000s
Balance adjustment

Smoothly scale your total withdrawal based on portfolio balance: below the floor, spending ramps down toward the max cut (reached at $0); above the ceiling, it ramps up by the boost rate for every extra multiple of the ceiling — with no upper cap. Between the two, spending is unchanged.

Floor Balance Max Cut
000s
%

0 = no floor

Ceiling Balance Boost Rate
000s
%

0 = no ceiling

Goal Seek

Search for the highest sustainable spending plan instead of simulating fixed numbers. Check Include in search on settings below to choose what Goal Seek can tune.

Minimum acceptable chance the plan hits both targets below.

Max lifetime spending shortfall vs. plan in bad markets. 0% = stay on plan; higher = bigger plan, more belt-tightening allowed. Minimum withdrawals still protect essentials.

000s

Balance to leave behind at the end of the horizon, on top of never running out.

Success Rate (on plan)

Explore specific paths

A 3D plot of representative paths from the 5th to 60th percentiles. Column height represents portfolio balance, color reflects the annual market return for that year (Red = crash, Green = boom). Drag to rotate, scroll to zoom, right-drag to pan. Single-click a column to pin that simulation and mouse over each year for values; click the column again or empty space to release. Double-click a column to explore ~200 nearby simulations; double-click again to return.

Average Timelines

Shows portfolio balance and dynamic withdrawals over time for the 60th through 5th percentile paths. Point size and shape reflect each year's nominal return.
